title: Zapier action to connect multiple items to board
excerpt:
I have a Zap with an action step to create a new item on monday. One of the columns in the monday board is a ‘connect board’ column. 
When I map a list of ids to the Connect Board column, I get this error when I create t…

posts.author: PrintSimple (Dave Fiorante)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a Zap with an action step to create a new item on monday. One of the columns in the monday board is a ‘connect board’ column.

When I map a list of ids to the Connect Board column, I get this error when I create the item:

Failed to create an item in monday.com
Invalid value for undefined on undefined. There are items that are not in the connected boards

When I map only one id, it works and creates the item accordingly.

I can confirm that all items that I’m trying to connect are in the corresponding board, and based on this documentation, it should work…

Support

Zapier JSON Formatting Examples

When creating a new Zapier integration that utilizes monday.com to either create a new item or fill in column values, some columns will require the data you are sending to be set within a JSON form...

See recording below;

I appreciate any feedback you may be able to provide.

Thanks,
Dave

posts.author: corpo (Corpo)
posts.cookedHtml: I am experiencing the same issue as Dave - I can take two IDs that work individually, but after throwing them in an array separated by a comma (with or without a space after the comma) I get the same error. About to break this out into a direct API call to Monday, but sure would be nice if it worked in the Zap step properly.

posts.author: iwiretech (Brienne Martin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello! I stumbled upon my search to a solution to this problem over a year later! But, i found a solution that I wanted to post here in case anyone needs one. Use the Looping by Zapier to run through each iteam as an iteration. I had the best success adding them as text and separating them by a comma. I have attached pitures for you to reference.

Hope this helps!

posts.author: GreenieStuga (Gareth Green)
posts.cookedHtml:
​

Hi,

I wonder if anyone has had the same issue as me when trying to add multiple connections to a connected board column.

Use case: I have a google sheet of data I need to upload to a monday board which has one to many relationships across a pair of boards. I run an engineering company, one board contains parts, the other contains machine types. Each subassembly is used on one or more machine types.

I can add a single connection with no problems, just by adding the itemId. This is very standard practice for me, I have a lot of experience with linking zapier to Monday, so I know all the basics.

This works for both single items added to both these connect board columns:

542×480 20.6 KB

[image]

I believe the issue is to do with the format of the string I need to pass when there are multiple links. There is a very helpful article on this subject, which I followed to the letter:

Support

Zapier JSON Formatting Examples

When creating a new Zapier integration that utilizes monday.com to either create a new item or fill in column values, some columns will require the data you are sending to be set within a JSON form...

This article leads me to a JSON format, but it does not work:

[image]

533×135 7.99 KB

[image]

I have also tried:

7998983928,7998984911

{“item_ids”:[7998983928]} (To check if the JSON syntax works with a known id that is 100% valid )

All come back with the same error.

Has anyone encountered this and found a solution?

​

posts.author: EvoRestorations (EvoRestorations)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @GreenieStuga,

I Have tha same issues in this moment, Do you find any solution for this case?

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @GreenieStuga
Hi Dave,

When you need to connect multiple items to a Connect boards column via Zapier, the value has to be sent as a JSON array of item IDs. Even if you only have one ID, it still needs to be wrapped in brackets.

Example format
[1234567890, 9876543210]

If you are looping through items in Zapier, collect the item IDs first, then use Formatter by Zapier to create a line item or properly formatted JSON array before mapping it into the Connect boards column.
